title: English Literature Classics
description: Test your knowledge of classic novels, poets, and literary concepts.
subject: Literature

questions.explanation: Jane Austen wrote 'Pride and Prejudice', published in 1813. It follows the romantic development of Elizabeth Bennet.

questions.prompt: In Shakespeare's 'Hamlet', what is the name of Hamlet's father's ghost?
questions.explanation: The ghost is that of King Hamlet, Hamlet's father, who was murdered by Claudius.

questions.prompt: Which novel begins with the line 'Call me Ishmael'?
options: The Old Man and the Sea
questions.explanation: 'Call me Ishmael' is the opening line of Herman Melville's 'Moby-Dick' (1851).

questions.prompt: What is the narrative technique where the story is told from a first-person perspective called?
questions.explanation: First-person narration uses 'I' or 'we' as the narrator is a character in the story sharing their personal perspective.

questions.prompt: George Orwell's '1984' is set in which fictional superstate?
questions.explanation: The story is set in Oceania, one of three superstates in Orwell's dystopian world. The city of London (called Airstrip One) is where the protagonist Winston Smith lives.
